Title: Magnaporthe oryzae infection triggers rice resistance to brown planthopper through the influence of jasmonic acid on the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to form dihydroflavonols such as dihydrokaempferol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with rice resistance to brown planthopper and rice blast; naringenin also inhibited Magnaporthe oryzae growth in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced by MeJA and by TH12-associated treatments, consistent with promotion of resistance-associated flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsF3H expression was upregulated after MeJA treatment. 2. OsF3H expression was also upregulated in rice treated with TH12 followed by N. lugens. 3. The gene is associated with increased accumulation of resistance-related flavonoids under JA-associated induction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by MeJA; induced after TH12 followed by N. lugens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues under MeJA treatment and TH12/N. lugens treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae TH12 infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; Nilaparvata lugens infestation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsMYC2 (indirect regulatory context discussed, but direct binding not shown in this paper for OsF3H)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BGISEQ-500); accession NA; metabolite quantification by UPLC-QTRAP 5500
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive engineering target to enhance defense-associated flavonoid accumulation, but should be considered with pathway balance and branch competition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes complex regulation by OsMYC2 and mentions inhibitory effect on OsF3H in supplementary dual-luciferase context, but the central experimentally validated target in the main text is OsF3'H.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP75B-type flavonoid hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a 3'-hydroxyl group into flavonoid B-rings, converting naringenin to eriodictyol and/or dihydrokaempferol to dihydroquercetin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target-product intermediates and branch products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids are associated with resistance to brown planthopper and rice blast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of rice resistance to N. lugens and rice blast; its expression is repressed by OsMYC2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsF3'H expression did not increase after MeJA treatment unlike several other flavonoid genes. 2. OsF3'H was downregulated after TH12 followed by N. lugens treatment. 3. In osmyc2 mutants, OsF3'H expression was upregulated. 4. Prior evidence cited and current interpretation indicate OsF3'H negatively regulates resistance to N. lugens and rice blast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; mutant expression analysis; prior mutant phenotype cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated after TH12 followed by N. lugens; not induced by MeJA/TH12/N. lugens in the same direction as other flavonoid genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues under MeJA, TH12 and N. lugens treatments show opposite trend to many other flavonoid genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae TH12 infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; Nilaparvata lugens infestation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsF3'H promoter used in dual-luciferase reporter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BGISEQ-500); accession NA; metabolite quantification by UPLC-QTRAP 5500
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing OsF3'H may alter flavonoid branch composition and cause off-target shifts among flavonoid products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising negative target for knockout or repression to improve resistance to brown planthopper and rice blast, but flavonoid profile changes should be monitored
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly treats OsF3'H as a negative regulator of resistance and a direct target of OsMYC2.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to corresponding CoA esters, supplying flavonoid biosynthetic precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with defense against brown planthopper and rice blast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced by MeJA, supporting JA-mediated activation of flavonoid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os4CL5 expression increased after MeJA treatment. 2. Its induction accompanied increased accumulation of eriodictyol, naringenin and quercetin. 3. It supports the mechanistic link between JA signaling and the flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive target for boosting defense-related flavonoid flux in combination with regulatory engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally assayed in Results as a MeJA-responsive flavonoid-pathway gene linked to nutrient metabolite accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes chalcones to flavanones such as naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with rice defense against brown planthopper and rice blast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced by MeJA, consistent with JA-promoted flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCHI expression increased after MeJA treatment. 2. This induction coincided with elevated levels of eriodictyol, naringenin and quercetin. 3. The data support activation of the flavonoid pathway downstream of JA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive target to increase flavonoid precursor formation under inducible defense programs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was one of the flavonoid genes directly quantified and linked to JA-induced flavonoid accumulation in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; MYC2-type JA signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of OsF3'H; broader positive control within JA-responsive defense network
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis branch selection under jasmonic acid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses OsF3'H transcription by binding its promoter; many flavonoid genes are downregulated in osmyc2 mutant, indicating broader positive influence on pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major JA-pathway regulator connecting rice blast-induced JA signaling to resistance-associated flavonoid remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with resistance to brown planthopper and rice blast; naringenin suppresses fungal growth in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates rice resistance to N. lugens and rice blast, at least in part by repressing the negative resistance gene OsF3'H
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYC2 is induced by MeJA, TH12 and N. lugens treatments. 2. osmyc2 mutants show reduced resistance to both brown planthopper and rice blast. 3. OsMYC2 localizes to the nucleus. 4. OsMYC2 directly binds the OsF3'H promoter and represses its transcription, linking JA signaling to flavonoid-pathway regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 mutant phenotyping; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Responsive to MeJA; induced by TH12 and N. lugens
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues after MeJA, TH12 and N. lugens treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae TH12 infection; methyl jasmonate treatment; Nilaparvata lugens infestation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid / methyl jasmonate; rice blast infection; brown planthopper infestation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BGISEQ-500); accession NA; metabolite quantification by UPLC-QTRAP 5500
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for transient effector in dual-luciferase; native OsF3'H promoter used in reporter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alteration of OsMYC2 may broadly affect JA responses and cause pleiotropic defense-growth tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering broad biotic-stress resistance, but pleiotropic JA effects require careful tuning or inducible deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although MYC2 can also affect additional flavonoid genes, the direct main-text target validated here is OsF3'H.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: JAZ repressor protein in JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation within jasmonic acid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA signaling affecting defense-associated flavonoid responses indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts as a negative regulator of JA-mediated defense output; detailed direct metabolic targets not established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator of resistance-associated JA/flavonoid defense state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with defense against brown planthopper and rice blast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: osjaz1 mutant shows increased resistance to N. lugens and rice blast, indicating OsJAZ1 negatively regulates this defense-metabolic state
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAZ1 showed expression trends consistent under N. lugens and TH12 treatments. 2. osjaz1 mutant plants displayed stronger resistance to brown planthopper. 3. osjaz1 mutant plants also showed smaller rice blast lesions. 4. OsJAZ1 therefore negatively regulates resistance outputs linked to JA/flavonoid pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; sequence verification; transcriptome comparison
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Consistent expression trend after N. lugens and TH12 treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae TH12 infection; Nilaparvata lugens infestation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad JA-related pleiotropy if edited, but not measured in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function of this JA repressor may improve dual resistance to insect and fungal attack, though broader JA-associated tradeoffs should be evaluated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because mutant phenotypes were directly tested in Results.
